entry.title: Re: Firebase Authentication Error in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Hi @timokapanen 

Thank you so much.

entry.title: Re: SOAP Async MaterialDocumentCreateRequest_Async_In API...
entry.content:
To capture the response from MaterialDocumentCreateConfirmation_Async_Out_V2, you need to implement an inbound service interface in your BTP ABAP system (which acts like a service provider for S/4HANA’s outbound call).

1. Expose an Inbound Service in BTP ABAP

You need to generate a proxy for the confirmation WSDL in BTP ABAP, and then implement it to receive the async confirmation.

  • Go to your BTP ABAP Environment project (or ADT).

  • Create a new service consumption model using the WSDL for MaterialDocumentCreateConfirmation_Async_Out_V2.

    • Use Service Consumption Model → Create from WSDL.

  • This generates an interface and data types.

  • Implement a class that implements the generated interface.

2. Register the Inbound Interface in Communication Arrangement

You must configure this in Communication Arrangement in BTP ABAP (e.g., /n/SAP/COMM app).

  • Add your inbound service (MaterialDocumentCreateConfirmation_Async_Out_V2) as the receiver.

  • Make sure it’s available to S/4HANA (via Cloud Connector if on-prem).

3. S/4HANA: Set BTP Endpoint as the Consumer

In the S/4 system:

  • Use transaction SOAMANAGER.

  • Open the Consumer Proxy for MaterialDocumentCreateConfirmation_Async_Out_V2.

  • Set the endpoint URL of your BTP ABAP receiver (which will receive the response).

  • This endpoint is the one exposed via BTP ABAP Runtime.

entry.title: Struggling with Leadership and Management Tasks? Get CMI Level 3 Assignment Help Today
entry.content: What is the best way to structure a written assignment for CMI Level 3 in Principles of Management and Leadership? Can CMI Level 3 Assignment Help guide me through the correct format and assessment criteria?

entry.title: Re: Integration Suite - iSeries (AS400) DB2 JDBC connection
entry.content:
Hi Sven,

We've also attempted to connect to a DB2 Datasource (on iSeries) using the mentioned JDBC Adapter. You can download the driver if you have an IBM ID. We have deployed JDBC Driver of IBM 11.5.9 on our Integration Suite Tenant, configured the CloudConnector, and were able to connect from the network perspective. So far, so good.

We followed every hint to connect to a specific DB on iSeries via the JDBC driver, but in the end, it failed with a licensing error. After checking the license (which was okay), we spent a long time talking with IBM and SAP. SAP mentioned the roadmap item for AS400 Driver Support (Version1) which is no longer there ;-)).

Here is SAP's response from the Service Deck and Productmanagement:

We plan to include a roadmap item for generic support of DB drivers for the JDBC adapter on the Edge Integration Cell. This means that with this delivery, the customer would be able to upload any DB driver to connect to any customer DB. However, please note, this option will only be available for Edge Integration Cell, not in the Cloud. Another point is that we will probably have to move this item to 2025.

To be honest, I don't believe SAP will come up with a working solution for Integration Suite due to incompatibility issues with IBM's JDBC Driver, their license, the Hyperscaler, and the underlying Apache Camel combination. There are similar topics and pitfalls for other adapters like OFT2, X.400, which SAP promised a long time ago but postpones every three months on the roadmap.

We gave up using/waiting for a working solution and integrate the scenarios wrapping the DB2 Data with Webservices. Not nice - but works for our requirement.

Best regards

Christian

entry.title: Re: request kernel release 1433
entry.content: SAP Community is not SAP official voice. Please contact SAP Support directly.

entry.title: Re: Error : Could Not Determine mapping steps for message...
entry.content:
Hi Ravi,

Please check the response received from SFTP adapter. 

The PI server sends a request to an SFTP server, but the data flow is in the opposite direction, from the SFTP server to the PI server. In other words, the PI server reads files from the SFTP server (a process that is also referred to as polling).  Herein this case the response message is not reaching mapping step properly or is not in format expected by mapping.

Try investigating the MS payload by turning on logging/tracing for the scenario from ID advanced settings. 

 

Regards

Anupam

entry.title: BTE for parked document
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I have a question on BTE for savings of parked document.

I would like to input a certain value at the line item text when I post a parked document at FBV1 transaction.

I believe the substitution won't work for this case, so I decided to use BTE functionality.

I have develeoped a related source code for 'SAMPLE_INTERFACE_00002218' and 'SAMPLE_INTERFACE_00002213'.

The functions are successfully executed when the parked document was saved, but any value is not entered after savings the parked document.

Is there anyone who knows why the BTEs do not work?

Thank you in advance.

BR,

Chris

entry.title: SE71 Graphical Form Painter Error after SAP GUI 800 Patch 11 (64-bit)
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

After upgrading to SAP GUI for Windows 800 Final Release - 64-bit, file version: 8000.1.11.1161, build: 2263391, patch level 11, I encountered an issue when attempting to use the Graphical Form Painter in Transaction SE71.

Here’s the exact error message I get:

Graphical Form Painter could not be called (FORMPAINTER_CREATE_WINDOW, )

Message No. TD245

 
Diagnosis (from SAP message): An error occurred structuring the graphical Form Painter.

System Response: The system switches to the alphanumeric Form Painter.

This issue only started occurring after the recent GUI update. Has anyone else experienced this problem? Are there any known issues or compatibility settings I should check? I’d prefer to avoid opening an OSS message unless necessary.

Thanks in advance for your insights!

Best regards,
Jay Dalwadi

entry.title: Stammtisch Brisbane
entry.content:
Hello All,

Thank you for your patience as we worked to organise the first 2025 Stammtisch for the Brisbane SAP Community. We’re excited to finally bring everyone together!

Come connect with fellow professionals, share insights, and enjoy great conversations in a relaxed setting. Whether you're a long-time SAP expert or new to the community, this gathering is a fantastic opportunity to network and collaborate.

We look forward to seeing you!

entry.title: RV_MESSAGES_GET inside MM07MFB9_BUCHEN_AUSFUEHREN and XNAST assignment
entry.content:
Hi, experts.
Why does 'RV_MESSAGES_GET' initialize XNAST table.
Before calling xnast nad ynast tables are emty and at once it i start to execite this fm xnast is filling with data
Why it's happerning?

  CALL FUNCTION 'RV_MESSAGES_GET'
    EXPORTING
      msg_kappl     = 'ME'
    TABLES
      tab_xnast     = xnast
      tab_ynast     = ynast
    EXCEPTIONS
      error_message = 4.
  IF NOT sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
    MESSAGE ID sy-msgid TYPE a NUMBER sy-msgno WITH
               sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.
  ENDIF.

entry.title: Re: Integration of employee data to LMS
entry.content: We dont have EC but I am using the Integration Center to generate the user data file. Remember you need to create a EDI/Stacked integration with 2 headers if you want to use the SF User connector. If you are using User connector you can use the normal CSV file export. Let me know if you need anything else.

entry.title: Re: Preview/Print of a Document (Crystal Report) not working
entry.content:
Hi Andy,

on what version are those databases? And does this happen when first printing or after a while?

There are several possibilities:

- the database you are experiencing problems with is on the latest version, and the other one is an older, using 32-bit ODBC driver (new versions do not support 32-bit but only 64-bit)

- you are using FP2111 and are experiencing issues after printing several documents (see note 3151730 - Invalid Report File Path with Crystal Reports and/or note 3307252 - Invalid Report File Path in Document Printing or Emailing for explanation)

- there are some UDFs in the system tables which are not present in the database you are having problems with

Let me know if it is any of the above.

BR,

Matija

entry.content: The Latest Syntax that i am referring to is, the ABAP syntax that we use in classes to write optimized code.

entry.title: Re: Erroneous Authentication Popup when Previewing Fiori...
entry.content: Next time you can probably only delete cookies and not cache. If you can find the specific cookie causing the issue and delete only this one this is for sure even better.... Some of the cookies used for the dev space domain (and therefore are associated with the request) are also applicable for *.cloud.sap domain or other SAP BTP apps, so it can affect re-login to those. With that it should not affect non-SAP related browser tabs, so it is better than cleaning the global cache / cookies from my personal experience.

entry.title: TR deployment issue
entry.content:
February 2024: 1382 company code added to 93 GL accountant derived roles via TR1

April 2024: 1383-1389 (7 company codes) added to 96 GL accountant roles via TR2 ( all roles from TR1 are also in TR2)

TR1 went live after UT, SIT and UAT on 17th April 2024 for company code: 1382.

7 company codes 1383-1389 are also seen on the organizational changes of roles involved. TR2 UAT, SIT not yet completed. These are not seen listed on T001 table or on tcode OX02.

TR2 is NOT released to production, only TR1 has got released on 17th April.

Why has TR2 changes got deployed along with TR1 changes?

entry.title: Re: DISTINCT OCRD from OINV
entry.content:
Hi,

just alter the join to OCPR to be "OCPR T2 ON T1."CntctCode" = T2."CntctCode" AND T1."CardCode" = T2."CardCode".

BR,

Matija

entry.title: Datasphere Graphical View Filter WHERE IN
entry.content:
Hello,

When creating a SQL view its quite easy to write in a WHERE clause the code

WHERE Field_1 NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT FIELD_11 FROM ATABLE).

How would would one create this in a performant way in a Graphical view ?

I know you could Join 2 Tables and then FILTER the result on a field being Blank/Empty, but is that performant. At this moment I see no posibility to do this easily.

entry.title: Re: How to Manage Cross-System Segregation of Duties (SoD) Risk?

entry.title: Re: Attribute text not showing after deploying analytical...
entry.content:
Hi, you can create a dimension view for the employee, including all attributes and the corresponding text you need.

The sample dimension for Saler is as follows: ID is the key, Name is the text for this ID, it also has an attribute and Status and corresponding text "Status Text." You need to set the semantic type of the text column as Text and then associate it with the corresponding columns. 

In the Analytic model, you can't display the column whose semantic type is text, but you can display the text when drilling down to the corresponding key column.

entry.title: Re: Integration Suite - iSeries (AS400) DB2 JDBC connection
entry.content: Have you tried a detour via MS SQL Server? The JDBC connection from Cloud Integration to MSSQL works great with Microsoft JDBC Driver for SQL Server. If you have a MSSQL instance you can connect your IBMi via Linked Server.

entry.title: Now Define Variables from Pre-existing Schemas
entry.content:
SAP Build Apps now has the ability to create page variables based on existing schemas, like from other variables, data entities, and most interestingly, processes. This is really helpful.

I especially like that for every process type of schema, you can get both the input and output schemas -- more on this below.

HISTORICAL CONTEXT: I'm sure this feature was an idea for a while, but I first heard about when the new Processes flow functions were released a year ago. It was great that you could now trigger processes from SAP Build Apps without all the manual calling of an API, but you still had to create the variables for the inputs and outputs -- if you made one data type mistake the process would fail. So the answer was for the schemas to be easily inherited from the process, and now that is possible.

The Basics

When creating a new page variable, you can still define it from scratch, but now you can simply copy the schema from somewhere else:

Important to note that these definitions copy the schema onto the new variable. You can then modify the variable definition -- there is "linking".

  • Variables: There are many times I need multiple copies of the same data -- I want to modify the data but keep the original in a different variable.
  • Actions and Process: With SAP Build Apps now able to easily call actions and processes, it is helpful to not have to create your own variables to hold the returned data, something you had to do up until now.
  • Entities: This you could do with Data Variables, but there were times when you wanted to modify that data without disturbing the original data.

Use with Processes

I got to understand the feature because I recently updated tutorials for integrating SAP Build Apps with SAP Build Process Automation, and the ability to quickly create variables made the tutorial easier to understand and quicker to perform.

https://developers.sap.com/mission.build-workshop-sales-order.html

Triggering a Process

When triggering a process, all I had to do was create a page variable from the process schema, indicating:

  1. Base it on a process
  2. Use my process
  3. Use the schemas involved with the APIs for triggering a process
  4. Use the inputs of the process

One of the pains before was that essentially in SAP Build Apps, when defining the REST APIs for calling SAP Build Process Automations, you could only have one schema definition, either the input or the output. Now, I can create page variables for the inputs and for the outputs.

Here, I set up the page variable for the inputs of our process inputs, which is quite complicated.

To trigger the process, all I had to do was bind the page variable to the fields. Then, when I click the button to trigger, I drag a Trigger Process flow function, and set the Input Parameters to the context property of the page variable. No more setting object properties and each field separately.

I can still change the binding type to Object Properties and set the fields manually, or I can use a Formula to make modifications to the page variable. 

Retrieving Instances

Getting info about the process instances was even simpler, and the ability to create both input and output schemas helped.

This time I created a variable based on the Retrieve Process Instances schemas, and used the output schema.

This provided the standard fields that are part of every such API call.

Now all I had to do was bind the page variable to the UI. And in the logic, I just put the output of the Retrieve Process Instances flow function into the variable. Really easy.

It took really no time to be able to view the list of process instances.

Tutorials

Again, you can see the whole SAP Build Apps integration with SAP Build Process Automation in the revised sales order tutorials, which are now even faster to do, less than an hour to do both the process and the app. 

https://developers.sap.com/mission.build-workshop-sales-order.html

entry.title: Re: Does switching from exactly once to at least once sem...
entry.content:
Hi Angelo,

                 In exactly once mode of delivery, PI has to guarantee that there will be no duplication of message same flow. This needs a high level of monitoring   and checking content of the message every time it is delivered to the target system. Therefore Exactly once mode of service increases operational complexity  and hampers system performance.

On the other hand, at least once mode of delivery can deliver a message more than once, but no message should be lost. As Data duplication is not an issue therefore atleast once mode of delivery takes less system resource and reduces complexity.   

Thus at least once mode of delivery is preferable over exactly once mode if system performance is the criteria to decide. 

 

Regards

Anupam

entry.title: Re: Mass upload of Sales Text

entry.title: Re: Batch-specific / -neutral available quantity in storage type when picking for supply to producit
entry.content:
Hello castin,

Thanks for your response. Unfortunately, I have to admit that I still did not fully understand your example. So, let me try to rephrase.

First, you started with a PMR requesting 600 LB. On one source bin you had three batches on one pallet. Simplified available stock looks like this:

  • 500 LB Batch001, SLED 31.12.2025
  • 500 LB Batch002, SLED 31.12.2026
  • 500 LB Batch003, SLED 31.12.2027

The system proposed to pick 500 LB of Batch001. As you set up to round up, the system additionally proposes to pick 500 LB of Batch002. That’s it. But you wanted to let the system round up to the whole quantity on the pallet.

Second, you switched to a storage type with Avail. Qt. Batches set to batch-neutral. Additionally, I assume that you set up rounding to Round WT Quantity to Available Quantity. Is that correct? Next, it is open to me how you set Mixed Storage in Storage Bin/HU: Shelf Life Expiration Date on storage type level. This will influence how available stock looks like. I assume you set it to Allowed - Earliest Date Dominant. Thus, simplified available stock in the second case would look like this:

  • 1500 LB (no batch info as batch-neutral), SLED 31.12.2025

When creating the WT for your PMR requesting 600 LB, the system would round up to 1500 LB according to the available quantity. During WT confirmation the picker would have to specify the batches picked.

If there would be multiple stock items on different bins with different SLED (see comment on Mixed Storage in Storage Bin/HU: Shelf Life Expiration Date on storage type level above), the system can sort available stock based on their SLED depending on the stock removal rule – in your case FEFO. You can see the SLED of available stock in the app Warehouse Monitor using e.g. node Stock and Bin -> Available Stock.

Best regards, Tobias

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the Future of Applying!!

entry.title: Re: Premium Hub CoE – Implementing the New Invoice Status Update Interface/Functionality for SAP BN4

Huseyin

entry.title: Re: EWM Outbound delivery - how to update a custom status...
entry.content:
Hi,

I do not remember if I ever implemented status change outside delivery change. Usually for small stuff it is easier to use eew header/item extension (if there are no filed control and operation dependencies based of the Z status). In case of EEW you have it directly visible in several transactions. But if you go with a status you can try following:

( /SCDL/CL_SP_PRD_OUT ) ->/SCDL/IF_SP1_ACTION~EXECUTE

aspect = /scdl/if_sp_c=>sc_asp_head

And you need to have proper inparam for the execution

you can find some examples in EWM in where used list of the /scdl/if_bo_action_c=>sc_set_status

I hope it helps,

BR,

Daniil

entry.title: RSP create duplicate instance backups
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am having an issue where 2 and sometimes 3 instance backups are created. 
RSP Backups are enabled:



For the past week it has been creating 2 instance backups daily, and yesterday 3 backups. 
It has happened before, but then goes back to creating a single backup daily. I am not sure what is causing this. 
I updated the RSP about a month ago to the latest patch, hoping it would resolve the issue. But it hasn't, and the schema backups stopped working as well. (scheduled the backups in the SLD now) 

Any help is always appreciated.
Thank you all,
Jerusha

entry.title: Re: How to handle generic JSON nodes with XCO?
entry.content: Hi, Is there a reasen that you aren't using the class /UI2/CL_JSON? I don't know the class you are using but I think you can assign the returning parameter with the inline declaration? When I am facing such problems I use the classes cl_abap_datadescr, cl_abap_tabledescr... and so on. This allows me to resolve and assign the structures recursively so I can access them. I hope that helps!

entry.title: Not able to release a sales order
entry.content:
Not able to release a sales order, system shows message 'some sales orders cannot be released; check document status.'

Steps:

  1. Go to the sales orders work center view.
  2. Search and select the order ABC.
  3. Click Action - Release, you get the error: some sales orders cannot be released; check document status.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Not able to release a sales order
entry.content:
The sales order ABC is with status=Open, which means the order has been released already. 

To further delivery the order, the user need to release the customer demand and then it will start the delivery process of the order.

SEC_OP2022.pdf

entry.title: Re: SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework
entry.content: Hello @Rene_de_Daniel, you have communicated on an updated SAP EA methodology Metro Map II on Linkedin, can you share-it with us in a post explaining the evolutions (I searched but did not find it)?

entry.title: Re: RV_MESSAGES_GET inside MM07MFB9_BUCHEN_AUSFUEHREN and...
entry.content:
Hello @Shizofrenik 

That is how RV_MESSAGES_GET is implemented. It initializes both TAB_XNAST and TAB_YNAST at the very beginning:

FUNCTION RV_MESSAGES_GET.
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*"*"Lokale Schnittstelle:
*"  IMPORTING
*"     VALUE(MSG_KAPPL) LIKE  NAST-KAPPL DEFAULT SPACE
*"     VALUE(MSG_OBJKY_FROM) LIKE  NAST-OBJKY DEFAULT ' '
*"     VALUE(MSG_OBJKY_TO) LIKE  NAST-OBJKY DEFAULT ' '
*"  TABLES
*"      TAB_XNAST STRUCTURE  VNAST
*"      TAB_YNAST STRUCTURE  NAST
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------

  REFRESH: tab_xnast,
           tab_ynast.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Batch managing in SAP B1
entry.content:
Dear @Felipe_Lima, I have more questions regarding this issue: how can I get this "stock posting list by production order..."? I don't have this option.

I get only  "inventory posting list by production order", without any batches.

 

Thank you in advance,

Marina

entry.title: Re: S4Cloud: Adobe Form - custom field hardcoded text not...
entry.content: Hello Jennifer - I can't paste a screenshot here, or don't see how. The Payer party referred to in the piece of script is derived from a field in an underlying subform. It comes from data binding <bind match="dataRef" ref="$.PayerParty.Partner"/>.

entry.title: Re: Pricing table error
entry.content: Hello Ramesh, what was the process/steps in activating the table. Thanks Abhishek

entry.title: Beyond Technology: Humane-Led Business Transformation in SAP Project Management
entry.content:
Introduction

In today's complex enterprise landscape, SAP implementations and transformations represent some of the most significant investments an organization can make. These projects not only touch nearly every aspect of a business's operations but also determine its competitive position for years to come. At the heart of successful SAP initiatives lies an often underappreciated but critically important component: the Project Management Office (PMO).

The SAP PMO serves as the strategic command center for these transformative journeys, orchestrating resources, timelines, methodologies, and stakeholder expectations. As organizations continue to navigate digital transformation challenges, the role of the PMO has evolved from a purely administrative function to a strategic powerhouse that drives business value realization.

This evolution brings us to a new paradigm in SAP project management: Humane led Business Transformation with Data-Driven Platforms. This approach recognizes that while technology and data are essential drivers of transformation, the human element remains central to success. The webinar "A Better Way To Run SAP Projects - The New Normal" highlights the importance of finding new approaches to manage SAP implementations more effectively, and this article will explore how combining human-centered leadership with data-driven insights creates a powerful framework for success.

This article explores the evolving landscape of SAP Project Management Offices—examining their critical functions, best practices, challenges, and how a human-centric, data-driven approach is reshaping the way they operate. We'll conclude with an in-depth look at how KTern.AI is revolutionizing SAP PMOs through AI-driven capabilities that amplify human potential rather than replace it.

The Human Element in SAP Project Management

Shifting from Technology-Centric to Human-Centric Approaches

Traditional approaches to SAP implementations have often prioritized technical considerations over human factors. However, experience has shown that technical excellence alone doesn't guarantee project success. The most sophisticated SAP solution will fail to deliver value if the people meant to use it aren't properly engaged, trained, and motivated to adopt new ways of working.

A humane-led approach to SAP transformation prioritizes:

  1. Stakeholder Engagement: Creating genuine buy-in rather than mandated compliance
  2. Change Management: Supporting people through the psychological journey of transformation
  3. Knowledge Building: Developing internal capabilities that enable self-sufficiency
  4. Cultural Alignment: Ensuring that technical solutions match organizational culture and values
  5. User Experience: Designing processes and interfaces with the human experience in mind

Data-Driven Decision Making: The Essential Complement

While the human element is primary, data provides the compass that guides transformation efforts. In modern SAP project management, PMOs leverage data to:

  • Identify bottlenecks and obstacles before they impact timelines
  • Measure user adoption and engagement in real-time
  • Quantify business impact rather than just technical completion
  • Allocate resources based on value creation potential
  • Make course corrections with confidence

The integration of humane leadership with data-driven decision making creates a powerful synergy—one that balances empathy and analytics, heart and head, people and performance metrics. This is what makes the "A Better Way To Run SAP Projects" approach so compelling—it recognizes that data and humanity are complementary forces rather than competing priorities.

The Modern SAP PMO: Strategic Partner in Business Transformation

Redefining the PMO's Role and Value Proposition

The SAP PMO has evolved dramatically from its origins as a project administration center. In today's environment, the PMO functions as a strategic partner in business transformation:

  1. Business Value Orchestrator: Ensuring that technical implementations translate into measurable business outcomes
  2. Transformation Facilitator: Guiding the organization through complex change
  3. Cross-Functional Connector: Breaking down silos between IT and business units
  4. Innovation Catalyst: Identifying opportunities to leverage SAP capabilities for competitive advantage
  5. Knowledge Hub: Building institutional expertise that drives self-sufficiency

The modern PMO is no longer just the project police—it's the transformation enabler that makes success not just possible but probable.

Key Functions of the Modern SAP PMO

The scope of SAP PMO responsibilities continues to expand, driven by the increasing complexity of SAP solutions and the accelerating pace of business change:

  • Strategic Alignment: Ensuring SAP initiatives support broader business objectives
  • Governance and Methodology: Establishing frameworks that balance structure with agility
  • Resource Orchestration: Optimizing the deployment of specialized skills
  • Change Management: Guiding the human side of transformation
  • Value Tracking: Measuring and communicating business benefits realized
  • Knowledge Management: Capturing lessons learned and building organizational capability
  • Vendor Coordination: Managing the ecosystem of implementation partners

Critical Success Factors for Humane-Led, Data-Driven SAP PMOs

Executive Sponsorship and Human-Centric Governance

Successful SAP PMOs operate with strong executive sponsorship that embraces the human element of transformation. Without leaders who prioritize people alongside technology, projects may be completed but transformation will fail.

A human-centric governance framework typically includes:

  • Executive steering committees with representatives who understand both technical and human factors
  • Clear roles and responsibilities with accountability for both technical delivery and user adoption
  • Transparent decision-making processes that incorporate stakeholder feedback
  • Regular governance meetings that address both progress metrics and human experience
  • Balanced scorecards that track technical completion, business impact, and user adoption

Integrated Change Management

Change management is no longer an afterthought but is integrated into every aspect of project planning and execution:

  • Stakeholder analysis that identifies impacts and resistance points early
  • Communication strategies tailored to different audience needs and concerns
  • Training approaches that go beyond feature/function to focus on new ways of working
  • Change impact assessments incorporated into technical design decisions
  • Change readiness metrics tracked alongside technical progress indicators

Data-Informed Resource Management

In the new normal of SAP project management, resource management goes beyond spreadsheets and utilization targets:

  • Skills inventories that capture both technical capabilities and soft skills
  • Capacity planning informed by actual performance data rather than theoretical estimates
  • Resource allocation protocols that match team members to roles that leverage their strengths
  • Training and knowledge transfer approaches based on learning style data
  • Work-life balance metrics to prevent burnout and maintain team health

Continuous Learning and Improvement

A distinguishing characteristic of mature SAP PMOs is their commitment to knowledge building and continuous improvement:

  • Systematic capture of lessons learned through project retrospectives
  • Communities of practice that foster knowledge sharing across teams
  • Performance benchmarking against industry standards and previous projects
  • Experimental approaches that encourage innovation in project delivery
  • Feedback loops that incorporate insights from all levels of the organization

Common Challenges in SAP Transformation and the Human-Centric Response

Despite best intentions, SAP implementations often encounter challenges that can derail progress. A human-centered, data-driven approach offers new ways to address these perennial issues:

Stakeholder Alignment and Expectations Management

Stakeholder alignment remains one of the most common points of failure in SAP projects. The humane-led approach addresses this challenge by:

  • Developing stakeholder journey maps that anticipate concerns and questions
  • Creating personalized communication plans based on stakeholder needs and preferences
  • Establishing regular touchpoints calibrated to appropriate frequency and detail level
  • Leveraging data visualization to make complex information accessible to non-technical stakeholders
  • Building consensus through facilitated workshops and collaborative decision frameworks

Technical Complexity and Integration

The technical aspects of SAP implementations present unique challenges that benefit from a blended human-data approach:

  • Using clear, non-technical language to explain complex concepts to business stakeholders
  • Creating visual representations of technical architectures and integration points
  • Balancing standard SAP processes with legitimate business-specific requirements
  • Ensuring testing scenarios reflect actual human workflows, not just technical paths
  • Collecting and analyzing user feedback on technical design decisions

Balancing Speed and Quality with Human Constraints

In today's fast-paced business environment, SAP PMOs face constant pressure to accelerate implementation timelines:

  • Implementing agile approaches adapted for SAP contexts that maintain sustainable pace
  • Prioritizing functionality based on both business value and human adoption readiness
  • Developing testing strategies that ensure quality without overwhelming teams
  • Creating realistic timelines that account for human factors like learning curves and change absorption
  • Monitoring team wellbeing metrics alongside progress indicators

Leveraging KTern.AI for Humane-Led, Data-Driven Transformation

The integration of human-centered leadership with powerful data analytics represents the frontier of SAP PMO evolution. KTern.AI embodies this integration, offering a comprehensive digital workspace that enhances human capabilities rather than replacing them.

Comprehensive Human-Centered Dashboard Ecosystem

KTern.AI's dashboard ecosystem serves the diverse needs of stakeholders at every level, providing the right information in the right format to the right people:

  1. Portfolio-Segment Dashboard: Provides PMO Heads, IT Directors, VPs, and CIOs with strategic visibility into phase-wise completion status, planned versus actual metrics, and segment statistics. This executive-level view ensures alignment between implementation activities and strategic business objectives, helping leaders make informed decisions that balance technical progress with organizational readiness.
  2. Executive Leadership Dashboard: Tailored specifically for VPs and CIOs, this dashboard delivers an executive summary that cuts through complexity to highlight what matters most. Executives don't need all the details—they need clarity on business impact and critical decision points. The KTern.AI executive dashboard delivers precisely this clarity.
  3. Project Dashboard: Project Managers, Testing Managers, and IT Managers gain real-time insights into work item status, resource utilization, and comprehensive tracking of tasks, issues, and test cases. This operational view drives day-to-day excellence through actionable metrics that balance progress tracking with human factors like workload distribution and capacity constraints.
  4. Phase Cockpit: Provides phase-wise project progress visualization that enables strategic oversight throughout the implementation lifecycle. This ensures stakeholders can track progress against key milestones while maintaining awareness of team performance and adoption readiness.
  5. My Workspace: Over 100+ project members benefit from a personalized workspace that centralizes their tasks, issues, test cases, sign-offs, user stories, risks, and CTAs. This human-centered design recognizes that people perform best when they have clarity on their specific responsibilities within the broader context of the project.

 

Advanced Analytics for Informed Decision Making

KTern.AI transforms raw project data into actionable insights that support human decision-making:

  1. Process Analytics: Project Managers, Testing Managers, and IT Managers gain visibility into process owners, process levels, and Line of Business status. These insights enable process optimization without losing sight of the human impact of process changes.
  2. Quality Assurance Analytics: Testing Managers and PMO Heads can monitor total test cases, overdue work items, defects, and resource utilization. This comprehensive view helps ensure quality outcomes while balancing workload across team members to prevent burnout.
  3. Project Pulse: PMO Heads and IT Directors can track key metrics including project start and end dates, along with progress percentages based on task count, weightage, and milestones. This holistic view of project health enables proactive management of both technical progress and team wellbeing.
  4. Milestone Statistics: PMO Heads gain in-depth analysis of all project milestones, facilitating strategic planning while ensuring that critical path activities receive appropriate attention and resources without overwhelming teams.
  5. Tasks-Insights: Project Leads gain phase-wise insights for each project member, enabling focused leadership and informed resource allocation decisions. This granular visibility supports effective team management and workload balancing.

Collaborative Tools that Enhance Human Interaction

KTern.AI enhances rather than replaces human collaboration through tools designed to make teamwork more effective:

  1. Signoff-Summary View: Project stakeholders gain visibility into parent and child signoffs through an intuitive pyramid view. This clarity around approval status and stakeholder responsibilities optimizes decision-making while ensuring accountability is distributed appropriately.
  2. Auto-Generated Reports: The platform automatically generates meeting reports, CTA tracking, issue reports, and comprehensive workitem documentation. This automation reduces administrative burden while improving communication transparency—a key aspect of human-centered project management.
  3. Event Insights: PMO Heads and IT Directors gain visibility into session details and attendance percentages, maximizing the impact of project events and ensuring effective use of collaborative time. Well-managed meetings create value rather than consuming it.
  4. Portfolio Groups: The platform offers customizable portfolio views that foster seamless collaboration across all project roles while maintaining strategic alignment for executive stakeholders. This flexibility allows teams to organize information in ways that best support their specific needs and working styles.

Transformative Impact on SAP PMO Capabilities

By implementing KTern.AI as part of a humane-led, data-driven approach, organizations can transform their SAP PMO capabilities in several key ways:

  1. Enhanced Visibility with Human Context: The platform provides unprecedented visibility into all aspects of SAP implementations while maintaining awareness of the human factors that influence success. This enables more compassionate and effective leadership throughout the transformation journey.
  2. Accelerated Execution with Sustainable Pace: Streamlined workflows and automated reporting reduce administrative overhead, enabling teams to focus on value-adding activities without creating unsustainable workloads or burnout conditions.
  3. Improved Quality Through Balanced Oversight: Specialized analytics for quality assurance help identify potential issues early while ensuring that quality gates are met consistently—without creating a culture of micromanagement or excessive pressure.
  4. Optimized Resource Utilization with Wellbeing Focus: Detailed insights into resource allocation enable more effective capacity planning while helping leaders ensure that specialized SAP resources are neither underutilized nor overwhelmed.
  5. Better Stakeholder Alignment Through Transparent Communication: Customizable dashboards and automated reports ensure that all stakeholders remain informed and aligned throughout the implementation journey, creating the transparency that is essential for trust-based collaboration.
  6. Knowledge Building for Long-Term Success: By centralizing project information and providing structured repositories for documentation and insights, KTern.AI helps organizations build institutional knowledge that benefits both current implementations and future initiatives.
  7. Data-Informed Decisions with Human Wisdom: The platform's analytics capabilities transform raw project data into actionable insights without removing human judgment from the equation—creating a balanced decision-making approach that is critical to the "new normal" of SAP project management.

Real-World Impact: Humane-Led, Data-Driven Transformation in Action

Organizations that have embraced the humane-led, data-driven approach to SAP transformations with KTern.AI report significant improvements across multiple dimensions:

  • Accelerated Time-to-Value: Implementation timelines reduced by 20-30% through improved coordination and proactive issue resolution
  • Higher User Adoption Rates: 40-50% increase in user adoption during initial go-live phases due to better change management and stakeholder engagement
  • Resource Optimization: 25% improvement in resource utilization through better capacity planning and workload balancing
  • Quality Improvements: 35% reduction in critical defects through enhanced testing coordination and quality analytics
  • Team Satisfaction: Significant improvements in project team satisfaction and reduced burnout, leading to better retention of SAP talent

As highlighted in discussions about "A Better Way To Run SAP Projects," the combination of human-centered leadership with data-driven insights doesn't just change project management metrics—it transforms how teams experience the implementation journey.

The Webinar Connection: Insights on "A Better Way To Run SAP Projects - The New Normal"

The webinar "A Better Way To Run SAP Projects - The New Normal" featuring Jan Musil explores several themes that align with the humane-led, data-driven approach facilitated by KTern.AI:

  1. Reimagining Project Delivery: The webinar discusses how traditional approaches to SAP project management are being challenged by new methodologies that place greater emphasis on human factors alongside technical considerations.
  2. The Importance of Data: A key theme is how data-driven insights can help project leaders make more informed decisions while still maintaining focus on the people who will ultimately determine project success.
  3. Adaptability and Resilience: The discussion highlights how the "new normal" of SAP project management requires greater adaptability and resilience—qualities that are enhanced when teams have both reliable data and human-centered leadership.
  4. Technology as Enabler: Rather than positioning technology as the driver of transformation, the webinar positions digital tools like KTern.AI as enablers that amplify human potential and create space for more strategic thinking.

These insights from the webinar reinforce the value proposition of KTern.AI as a platform that supports the integration of human wisdom with data-driven decision making.

Conclusion: The Human Future of Data-Driven SAP Transformation

As organizations continue to invest in SAP transformations to drive digital innovation and competitive advantage, the most successful will be those that embrace what might truly be called "the new normal"—an approach that puts people at the center while leveraging the power of data and technology to amplify human potential.

The future of SAP PMO excellence lies in this thoughtful integration of human wisdom and digital capabilities—combining the empathy, creativity, and adaptability of experienced professionals with the data processing power, analytical capabilities, and process automation of platforms like KTern.AI.

Organizations that achieve this integration will not only deliver more successful SAP implementations but will also build more resilient, capable teams and more adaptive cultures. In an increasingly complex and fast-changing business environment, this humane-led, data-driven approach to transformation may be the most significant competitive advantage an organization can develop.

By leveraging KTern.AI's comprehensive capabilities within a human-centered transformation philosophy, SAP PMOs can transcend their traditional role as project administrators and emerge as true catalysts for business evolution—driving not just successful implementations but meaningful human progress and sustainable business success.

The technology will continue to evolve. But the fundamental principle remains constant: successful transformation happens when people are empowered by data, not replaced by it. KTern.AI embodies this principle, offering a path to SAP transformation that is both more humane and more effective.

entry.title: 🚀 SAP AI QuickLaunch Series / 秒速で学ぶ SAP AI Agent 開発 🚀 - Part 1 Env Setup / 環境構築
entry.content:
Generative AI has leapt from research papers to daily business reality— and SAP is surfing that wave at full speed. In this hands‑on series, I’ll show you how to spin up a custom AI agent on SAP AI Core in minutes, then grow it into a production‑ready asset—without drowning in theory.

生成 AI は研究室の外へ飛び出し、今やビジネス現場の常識を塗り替えています。SAPは全速力でその波に乗っています。このブログシリーズでは、SAP AI Core の既定モデルを最速で呼び出し、実務で使える AI エージェントへ拡張する“秒速ハンズオン”をお届けします。

 

📖What You’ll Learn in This Series / 本シリーズで学べること

  • How to spin up a custom AI agent on SAP AI Core in minutes
    SAP AI Core 上でカスタム AI エージェントを “秒速” で動かす方法

  • Hands‑on with LangChain, Google Search Tool, RAG, and Streamlit
    LangChain・Google 検索ツール・RAG・Streamlit を使った実装

  • Exposing the agent as a REST API and rebuilding the UI in SAPUI5/Fiori
    エージェントを REST API 化し、UI を SAPUI5/Fiori に載せ替える手順

Time Commitment / 学習時間
Each part is designed to be completed in 10–15 minutes.
各章は 10–15 分 で読める＆手を動かせるを予定しています。

 

🗺️ Series Roadmap / 連載ロードマップ

  • Part 0 Prologue / プロローグ 
  • Part 1 Env Setup: SAP AICore & AI Launchpad / 環境構築: SAP AI CoreとAI Launchpad [current blog / 現在のブログ]
  • Part 2 Building a Chat Model with LangChain / LangChain でチャットモデルを構築
  • Part 3 Agent Tools: Integrating Google Search / Agentツール: Google 検索を統合
  • Part 4 RAG Basics ①: File Upload & HANA Cloud VectorEngine / RAG 基礎 ①: ファイルアップロード & HANA Cloud ベクターエンジン
  • Part 5 RAG Basics ②: Retriever Tool / RAG 基礎 ②: Retriever Toolの構築
  • Part 6 Streamlit UI Prototype / Streamlit でチャットUI
  • Part 7 Expose as a REST API / AI Agent を REST API 化
  • Part 8 Rebuild the UI with SAPUI5 / SAPUI5 でチャットUI を再構築

Note / 注記
Subsequent blogs will be published soon.
続編は順次公開予定です。

Env Setup: SAP AICore & AI Launchpad
環境構築: SAP AI CoreとAI Launchpad

1 | Overview / 概要

In this chapter, we’ll connect to SAP AI Launchpad using the service key created for the SAP AI Core instance. Then, we’ll proceed to deploy the LLM for running a chat model.

本章では、SAP AI Core インスタンスに対して作成したサービスキーを使い、SAP AI Launchpad に接続します。その後、チャット用 LLM を動かすためのデプロイメントを実施します。

 

2 | Prerequisites / 事前準備

  • BTP sub-account / BTP サブアカウント
  • SAP AI Core instance / SAP AI Core インスタンス
  • SAP AI LaunchPad Subscription / SAP AI Launchpadのサブスクリプション
  • Python 3.13 and pip / Python 3.13環境 & pip
  • VSCode, BAS or any IDE / VSCodeやBASなどのIDE

 

3 | Step 1 – Create a Service Key / Service Key の作成

Creating a service key gives your local scripts a secure, OAuth‑based passport into SAP AI Core. Without it, Python SDK calls have no way to authenticate or discover endpoints.

サービスキーは、ローカルのスクリプトが SAP AI Core に安全かつ OAuth ベースでアクセスするための “パスポート” です。これが無いと、Python SDK はエンドポイントや認証情報を取得できません。

  1. Open your AI Core instance in BTP Cockpit → Instances & Subscriptions.
    BTP Cockpit の Instances & Subscriptions で AI Core インスタンスを開きます。

  2. Go to the Service Keys tab and click Create. Give it any name.
    Service Keys タブで Create をクリックし、任意の名前を入力して作成します。

  3. Download the generated JSON; you’ll need it later.
    生成された JSON をダウンロードして保管します。

NOTE – Resource Group
When calling SAP AI Core from Python you must set an extra environment variable named RESOURCE_GROUP in addition to the values contained in the service key. In this tutorial we’ll simply point it to the built‑in default group.
Python から呼び出す際にはサービスキーに含まれている変数以外に Resource Group と呼ばれる環境変数が必要ですが、本チュートリアルでは既定の default を指定します。

 

4 | Step 2 – Connect AI Launchpad / AI Launchpad との接続

You can deploy an LLM (Large Language Model) directly to SAP AI Core using the service key you created earlier. However, in this guide, we'll use SAP AI Launchpad to make the deployment process easier.
To begin, you'll need to establish a secure connection route from SAP AI Launchpad to SAP AI Core.

先ほど作成した SAP AI Core のサービスキーを使えば、SAP AI Core に直接アクセスして LLM（大規模言語モデル）をデプロイすることも可能です。ただし、ここではより簡単な方法として「SAP AI Launchpad」を使ってデプロイを行います。まずは、SAP AI Launchpad から SAP AI Core へ安全に接続するための経路を準備しましょう。

  1. From Subscriptions open AI Launchpad → Go to Application.
    Subscriptions から AI Launchpad → Go to Application で起動します。

  2. Click Add (top‑right) → API Connection and upload the Service Key JSON.
    右上の Add → API Connection を選び、Service Key JSON をアップロードします。

  3. Select the default Resource Group in the side panel and save.
    サイドパネルで Resource Group に default を選択して保存します。

 

5 | Step 3 – Configure & Deploy an LLM / モデル設定とデプロイ

On the "Configuration" screen of SAP Launchpad, you can select the type of foundation model to use. Think of this as creating a model profile that can be shared across multiple deployments.

Let’s go ahead and create the configuration. In this case, we’ll use the default foundation_models scenario and configure it to use gapt-4o-mini.

SAP Launchpad の「設定」画面では、使用する基盤モデルの種類などを選びます。これは、複数のデプロイメントで共有できる “モデルのプロファイル” のようなものと考えると理解しやすいでしょう。

それでは設定を作成してみましょう。今回は、デフォルトで提供されている foundation_models というシナリオを選択し、gapt-4o-mini を指定します。

  1. Navigate ML Operations → Settings and click Create.
    左メニュー ML 運用 → 設定 で 作成 をクリックします。

  2. Fill in: Name = <any‑name‑you‑like>, Scenario = foundation_models, Version = 0.0.1, Executable = azure-openai.
    設定名 <ご自由に>, シナリオ foundation_models, バージョン 0.0.1, 実行可能 azure-openai を入力します。

  3. On the parameter screen leave modelName = gpt-4o-mini, modelVersion = latest and click Next → Create.
    パラメータ画面では modelName = gpt-4o-mini, modelVersion = latest をそのままに 次へ → 作成。

  4. You’ll land on the new Setting detail page—check that all fields look correct; no extra edits needed.
    作成後に設定詳細の画面へ遷移します。入力内容に誤りがないかざっと確認しましょう（基本的にそのままで OK です）。

  5. Click Create Deployment (top‑right), breeze through the wizard by clicking Next until Create.
    右上の 「デプロイメント作成」 を押し、ウィザードは特に変更せず 「次へ」 → 「作成」 で進めます。

  6. Wait until Status = Running—hit the Refresh icon every few seconds. Copy the Deployment ID shown in the title bar for later use.
    ステータスが 「実行中」 になるまで、更新ボタンで数秒おきにリフレッシュします。タイトルバーに表示される Deployment ID を控えておきましょう。

By the way, there are various LLMs available to run on SAP AI Core. You can browse them in the Generative AI Hub > Model Library section.

ちなみに、SAP AI Core 上で利用可能な LLM はさまざまです。「生成 AI ハブ > モデルライブラリ」画面を開くと、それらの一覧が確認できます。

The Leaderboard also allows you to compare models based on accuracy, token cost, and other metrics.

また、リーダーボード機能では、各モデルの精度やトークンコストなどを比較することもできます。 

 

6 | Next Up / 次回予告

Part 2 Building a Chat Model with LangChain / LangChain でチャットモデルを構築

We’ll finally implement the chat model! Make sure your Python development environment is ready—VS Code or a similar IDE will work perfectly!

Part 2では、いよいよチャットモデルの実装に入ります！VS Code などを使って Python の開発環境を準備しておきましょう！

 

Disclaimer / 免責事項

Disclaimer – All the views and opinions in the blog are my own and is made in my personal capacity and that SAP shall not be responsible or liable for any of the contents published in this blog.
免責事項 – 本ブログに記載された見解および意見はすべて私個人のものであり、私の個人的な立場で発信しています。SAP は本ブログの内容について一切の責任を負いません。

entry.title: Re: Edit journal entry when processor approval Verify Gen...
entry.content:
Dear Jeremy，

Thank you very much for your prompt reply. I operated based on your instructions, but I couldn't find this setting in the second step

  • Check the option to “Allow changes to journal entry”

 The screenshot of our system is like this.

 

Please help guide me on where to find this setting. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Problem to connect Business Application Studio to S/4...
entry.content:
Hello Chris,

I raised a ticket with SAP Support. They applied a manual fix for our case and confirmed it won’t recur in the upcoming S/4HANA Public Cloud release.

Parat S.

entry.title: Inspection Task Notification to Quality User
entry.content:
Hi I need help for Quality process

Whenever a production confirmation and inspection task is generated the quality user is notified about the task. Quality user gets email or information.

I tried with workflow rules,

but there is no object available for the inspection task.

I also need workflow whenever goods are received user gets notified with quantity I tried to create workflow but user gets only warehouse receiving id no other details are entered like item code quantity warehouse id etc.

entry.title: Re: Cloud ERP - Opportunities, Challenges & SAP's Outreach
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ERP Private

The new SAP Cloud ERP Private package for existing SAP on-premise customers accelerates your cloud ERP transformation. It includes Business applications, Transformation tools and services, & Cloud optimization and extensibility services. It is based on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition in a private cloud operating model. For more details, refer Introducing SAP Cloud ERP Private Package to Accelerate Transformation.

The only option I see available is Technology Q&A. Kindly help.

 

Regards,

Divakar.

entry.title: SAP MDG Parallel processing in Mass processing of data
entry.content:
Hello Gurus,

We have a requirement to configure Consolidation and mass processing using Change Request for client, version 2023. For mass process, they have requirement to process multiple CRs for same entity ( which I understand is parallel processing). However when I am creating configID for activation of material using CR, I cannot select my CR type(which is configured for parallel processing). I am using copy of CR type MATL0A, Business Activity MATA. Kindly guide how can I achieve this requirement of activating material with CR for mass processing using parallel processing.

Thank you,

Shweta

But the hidden logic is working, correct? Just the assignement of the value doesn't work? The fieldlength is enough?

entry.title: 8 Baby Steps to Learn Joule - (Step #4) Joule Interaction Patterns
entry.content:
This blog is part of the series 8 Baby Steps to Learn Joule. Please check the previous blogs, before proceeding.

 #1 – Introduction to Joule

#2 How Joule Works

#3 Joule Architecture

 

In this blog, we will dive deep into Interaction Patterns in Joule.

 

Let’s first quickly recap what we learnt so far:

  • Joule is an advanced, generative AI copilot.
  • It is embedded into various SAP applications, for example SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors etc.
  • Users ask their queries in the natively integrated Joule client embedded in SAP cloud solutions, such as S/4HANA, SuccessFactors etc. 
  • Joule analyses the prompt, provides a much richer query to the LLM, and finally returns a secure, accurate, and appropriate response back to the user.
  • Technically, Joule is a service running on SAP BTP in a Cloud Foundry environment.
  • Users can access Joule through a diamond icon in the top right-hand corner of the SAP user interface, regardless of the specific system. 
  • Joule uses SAP Cloud Identity Services for authentication and authori

 

What is Interaction Pattern in Joule?

The interaction pattern refers to the different ways users can communicate with Joule to get information, perform tasks, or navigate across applications.

Joule supports 4 types of interaction patterns:

  1. Informational
  2. Navigational
  3. Transactional
  4. Analytical

Below image gives a quick glimpse on these 4 interaction patterns.

 

 

 

Informational

With informational interactions Joule provides knowledge-based results. These are, for example, policy related questions, information in SAP Help, or customer documents stored in third-party repositories.

Joule supports two categories of informational pattern:

  • One is based on SAP owned content with the goal to have all our knowledge-base available via Joule. This is managed by SAP and offered to every Joule user out of the box.
  • Additionally, SAP customers can upload their own content. For example, customers can upload their own HR policies, travel policies, information content, and others. This is called Document Grounding.

 

Example of Informational interaction pattern – in Joule with SuccessFactors

Prompt – “Tell me the birthdays of my team members.”

 

 

 

Prompt - “How many leaves do I have this year?”

 

 

Example of Informational interaction pattern – in Joule with S/4HANA

Prompt: "What is the approval process for purchase orders in our company?"

 

Navigational

In this interaction pattern, Joule interprets if the users want to navigate to a business application and navigate them directly where they want to go.

This is especially helpful for users who are not very familiar navigating SAP applications.

 

Example of navigational interaction pattern – in Joule with SuccessFactors

Prompt – “Show me my organization details”

 

 

Example of navigational interaction pattern – in Joule with S/4HANA

Prompt – “Show me all open sales orders.”.

Joule will show a list of open sales orders. It will also provide a link that redirects the user to the S/4HANA Manage Sales Orders app.

 

Transactional

With transactional pattern, Joule provides users direct entry point to SAP, the backend system, allowing them to do CRUD operation directly from Joule.

For example, review purchase order, create job positions, change address etc.

 

Example of transactional interaction pattern – in Joule with SuccessFactors

Prompt – “Change my address”

 

 

 

 

Analytical

SAP plans to introduce analytical interactions. This will enable analytical interactions based on JustAsk and SAP Analytics Cloud.

Example: “Show me the top five performing products by revenue this quarter."

 

This is in nutshell - Interaction Patterns in Joule!

 

What's Next?

Blog #5 - Joule in SAP SuccessFactors [Will be published soon]

Meanwhile, you may read the blog Joule, SAP’s Generative AI Copilot

entry.title: Re: Nested Post Calls in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:

Thank you for the reply,

I did try to post headers first and then separately post the sales order item(s), I was able to post the headers but while separately posting the sales order item(s) I receive " Request failed with status code 404".

I tried first with "create record" for sales order item(s) and then I tried the "update record" functionality for sales order item(s), I am unable to post in either of the ways.

Is there any way to post to both headers and the sales order item(s) which is nested in any certain way using sap build apps, can you please guide me in that way.

Regards,
Yashmin Behera

entry.title: Re: Post Goods receipt for service order
entry.content:
Hello again dear user,

I can finally not be coming from FSG as the fields configured are the same between YB29 and YB04.

Can you please provide screenshots when you proceed the MIGO step by step and ,in your PO item details ,under "Delivery" tab, check if the "Goods Receipt" checkbox is ticked. If your PO is account assigned, check if the Account assignement category is Goods receipt relevant.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Customer Self Service in SAP for Me - Cloud Purchase Order Edits
entry.content:
Updated on April 22nd, 2025, with new functionality.

We are pleased to announce our new functionality which goes live later on today, which gives you the opportunity to now edit your Purchase Order information for Cloud orders.

From the SAP for Me home page, navigate to Finance and Legal and the Cloud card.

And then go to the Contracts tab and on the right-hand side, Manage PO numbers.

This opens up a Filter page, where you can then see which contracts are available for editing with PO information. 

You can either select one or all of the relevant contracts.

Either enter in a brand new PO number or edit the existing one, and enter a validity date. The validity date option is up to five years out.

Once entered, then click on the Add New PO number and then click on the Confirm button and you will get a confirmation message.

 

You can also enter in that No PO is required.

And then you will get another confirm message to say  that this remains until the end of your contract, unless you chose to edit again.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud - Threshold Visualization Issue
entry.content:
Hi,

This happen sometime, you can try to Save-As the story and try again, might be it will work or you can follow below article on threshold and conditional formatting in SAC

https://sactutorial.com/sap-analytics-cloud-table-formatting/

entry.title: Re: RV_MESSAGES_GET inside MM07MFB9_BUCHEN_AUSFUEHREN and...
entry.content:
Basically, these tables are export parameters and not changing parameters. As any good programmer should do, they are initialised and populated in this GET data FM.

(The reason why TABLES parameters are considered obsolete)

entry.title: Fiori OVP Analytical Card - Error OData Property 'NETPRICEAMOUNTAGGREGATE' already exists
entry.content:
Hi,

we have an OData V4 Service and I want to create an OVP with analytical cards. OData is V4 via RAP - No special analytical annotations. 

For my measure NETPRICEAMOUNT I will get the following error on opening the app in the error log:

Property 'NETPRICEAMOUNTAGGREGATE' already exists in entity type 'ET_6FF73BF54300DA439E9DDB6337A'

The aggregate property seems to be automatically generated. Somebody any Idea? 

Best regards Richard

entry.title: Re: SAP User Experience Q1/2025 Update – Part 7: UI Design and Technology for Web and Mobile
entry.content:
Hi jvanattenhoven,

Launchpad Space Templates and Launchpad Page Templates is currently not planned to be available on SAP Fiori launchpad On-Premise.

Nevertheless, it has been possible to create Spaces and Pages based on Predefined Content in SAP Fiori launchpad On-Premise for a while now.

Best regards,
Aparna

entry.title: formal validation , the status is getting updated claim is posted instead of validation sucessful
entry.content: Customer claim , The formal validation is getting updated with the calim posted instead of Content validiation sucerssful . This is stoping me for part return. I need to reverse the posting and do the part retun. Need to understand where is it going wrong.

entry.title: REM - down payments for supplier and customer contracts (S/4 HANA Cloud)
entry.content:
Dear SAP experts,

we would like to know how down payments are handled in the REM module (S/4 HANA Cloud) for supplier and customer contracts. 

What conditions and what special ledger features can be used? We've already checked all SAP notes and relevant scope items but this information is not provided and we need this information for our customer who would like to use S4 HANA Cloud system for their leas in a lease out process.

Thank you,

Simona

entry.title: Re: BTE or substitution does not work druing FB70 simulation
entry.content:
We managed to solve the problem by creating a FMDERIVE rule. The difference was that, it required a different type of rule, not like the one we created before, although it filled the field with a value.

We created one with "Function Module", but actually it requires a simple "Derivation Rule"

Using the Rule we avoided the error message. After that the BTE 1120 filled the proper values.

Regards

entry.title: Re: Using a Function Module in SAP RAP managed
entry.content: WOW, that's even better i will definitely consider applying it on my next FM usage

entry.title: Re: How to implement SAP BTP Audit log service in a CAP Application

2.Now our service is multi-tenant architecture, before we use the CAP framework, we can choose the Auditlog store in provider subaccount or consumer subaccount. But we can't find where to store the Auditlog in CAP framework now. Do you know how to set the place that the Auditlog should store in there?

entry.title: Re: BTE for parked document
entry.content:
FI substitution call point 3 should work when posting a parked document.

  • Ref: 1745890 - Substitution by BADI AC_DOCUMENT is not processed for FI T-codes

entry.title: filter bars with Smart variant managements are not working in sapui5
entry.content:
hi experts,

I am encountering a weird scenario where I have 2 view (View 1, View 2) , with 2 different filter bars. ( I am using sap.ui.comp.filterbar & sap.ui.comp.smartvariants ) 

Both filter bars has different persistencyKey and smartVariantManagementId. 

i am referring to the sample code, to fetch, apply and register.

FilterBar in DynamicPage with a Table - Samples - Demo Kit - SAPUI5 SDK

When i save the filter variant in the view 1, i can see the saved variant in the view 2 filter bar

How can I get rid of this weird behaviour?

entry.title: In IW32 maintenance order _ Cost tab Report : List: Rep. Budget/Commit display issue
entry.content:
Hi 

 In maintenance order (IW33/IW32) business wants to view cost report in cost tab, when we select Rep. Budget/Commit in maintenance order there is no Pop raising for selection of multiple reports -
List: Plan/Actual/Committe
Orders: Actual/Plan/Variance 

Directly it reaching to Orders: Actual/Plan/Variance report screen. 

We need multiple selection screen and like to select List: Plan/Actual/Committe. This is not happening. 
This is working in DEV client, but not in PRD and QAS system.
We have followed both note: 2567068 - IW32 - No reports are assigned to transaction (KB702) & 146102 - No/other cost reports in PM/SM order 

In QAS 300 system we generated report 6OBU and after that S_ALR_87013019 report started working when we execute as transaction, but still, we are not getting multiple selection report pop up in maintenance order (IW33/IW32). 

Please suggest how to get multiple select report in maintenance order and to select List: Plan/Actual/Committe report through IW32/IW33.

entry.title: Re: JDBC Receiver: 'com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServe...
entry.content: Hi, the issue has been resolved. It wasn’t related to Integration Suite—most likely it was caused by either the Cloud Connector or the Database.

entry.title: Re: Object does not exist in Smartforms
entry.content:
Read this KBA 2267927 - Error message "CSapEditorCtrl::GetObject: Object <number> does not exist" with SAP GUI 740 Patch 5 or above 

  • I would suggest to apply steps of 742662 - MS Word as Editor in SAPscript and Smart Forms
